1. Distance: 10 miles

3. Remote/In-person: In-person
4. Quick Apply

3. Adjust the Distance to 10 miles

"Quick apply only" is now selected. Now let me select "On-site" for in-person jobs.

"On-site" is now selected. Now I need to adjust the Distance slider to 10 miles. I can see the distance slider. The current position seems to be at around the 4th notch. The distance is now set to 10mi. I can see the slider is at the 10mi position. Now I need to scroll down to check if there's a "Full-time" job type filter. Let me scroll down further in the filter dialog.
I can see "Date posted" and "Salary range" sections. I need to scroll more to find the job type (Full-time) filter. Let me continue scrolling down.
I can see the "Employment types" section with "Full Time" option. Let me select "Full Time".

"Full Time" is now selected. Now I have all four filters set:

Let me click "Apply Filters" to apply these filters.

The filters have been applied successfully. I can see the results now show **29 jobs in Bath, NY** with all the filters active:
- Quick apply only ✅
- On-site (in-person) ✅
- 10mi distance ✅
- Full Time ✅

The filter icon shows "4" indicating 4 active filters. The results show jobs with "Quick apply" badges and "On-site" labels. Let me scroll down to see more of the job listings and capture the full list.
I can see the first 20 results (page 1 of 2). Let me also check page 2 to get the complete list. Let me scroll down in the left panel to see if I can click on page 2.
Let me continue scrolling in the left panel to see the pagination and navigate to page 2.

I can see the footer of the page. It seems the page 2 pagination link was in the left panel above. Let me scroll back up to find it. I noticed earlier in the text extraction that there was "1 2 Showing results 1-20". Let me scroll up a bit to find the pagination.
I can see the pagination: page 1 (current) and page 2. It shows "Showing results 1-20" on page 1. Let me click on page 2 to see the remaining jobs.
Now I have all the data. Page 2 shows results 21-29, with 9 additional jobs. Combined with the 20 from page 1, that's 29 total jobs. Let me now submit the complete answer.
